title: Long-read DNA preparation for metagenomic samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f96t9","text":"General:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"19tik","text":"This is an DNA isolation protocol adapted to the properties of sludge samples from biogas and wastewater treatment plants. The protocol reliably retrieves metagenomic DNA of sufficient quality, length and yield. The performance has been validated on 20 different reactor plants. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6eb3e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b7gl8","text":"Observed Performance on 20 ONT runs using this protocol:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":56}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5nb4p","text":"It does not negatively impact the overall throughput for Nanopore Sequencing. Sequencing runs were active over 2.5 days, with one refuel step after 18 h.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fpupb","text":"N50 Ranges between 4000 and 8000 bp, depending on the sample type. E.g. frozen samples have a lower N50, fresh samples a higher N50. General throughput is between 18-28 Gigabases of a single flowcell (flowcell 106 Rev D, using the LSK-109 Kit).  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2r8v9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dhvog","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1v1nc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"du7ga","text":"Funding:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8ccda","text":"This work was funded by the Deutsche Forschungsgemeinschaft (DFG, German Research Foundation) - BR 5692/1-1 and BR 5692/1-2","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Add 1 ml of homogenic sludge/substrate via 10 ml syringe (without needle) to a 2 ml tube 1 mL, sludge/substrate
Centrifuge for 5 min at 20,000g and remove supernatant 00:05:00
Transfer sample to lysing matrix E tube and add 778 µl sodium phosphate buffer 778 µL, sodium phosphate buffer
Add 122 µl MT buffer, homogenize tube by hand 122 µL, MT buffer

Homogenize via FastPrep®-24 Classic I. for 20 s at 6 m/s and put directly on ice after lysis. 00:00:20 MP Biomedicals™ FastPrep -24™ Classic Instrument (Benchtop homogenizer, Fisher Scientific, 12079310, https://www.fishersci.co.uk/shop/products/mp-biomedicals-fastprep-24-instrument/12079310 ) The device may strongly influence your expected total yield and read lengths. Try different times and strengths first if you are using another device and analyse your DNA length distribution at the end, e.g. by agarose gel or Agilent Bioanalyzer, and determine the best settings for your device. 6 m/s corresponds to 3700 rpm, according to the manufacturer
Centrifuge for 15 min at 4 °C with 14,000g 00:15:00 4 °C
Transfer supernatant to a clean 2 ml tube
Add 1 µl RNase A to each sample and incubate for 5 min 1 µL, RNase A 00:05:00
Add 250 µl PPS (protein precipitation solution) and mix carefully but efficiently by inverting at least 10 times 250 µL, PPS
Centrifuge at 14,000 g for 10 min at 4 °C 00:10:00 4 °C
Transfer supernatant to clean 5 ml tube
Resuspend binding matrix suspension and add 1 ml binding matrix suspension to the 5 ml tube 1 mL, binding matrix suspension
Invert by hand for 2 min (avoid "matrix pellet" here) Place on a rack for 0.5 to 1 h 00:02:00 00:30:00, or up to 1 hour
Discard 650 µl supernatant without disturbing the binding matrix

Transfer approx. 600 µl of the mixture to a spin filter and centrifuge at 14,000g for 1 min 00:01:00 Empty the catch tube and repeat the steps until all the binding matrix is in the spin filter Empty the catch tube.

Add 500 µl HA-wash solution and gently resuspend the matrix by stirring using a pipette tip Centrifuge at 14,000 g for 1:00min, empty catch tube 500 µL, HA-wash solution 00:01:00 Slightly stir with the pipette tip, do not overdo resuspending, as well for the next step
Add 500 µl SEWS-M and gently resuspend the pellet using a pipette tip Centrifuge at 14,000 g for 1:00 min, replace the catch tube 500 µL, SEWS-M 00:01:00
Centrifuge a second time at 14,000 g for 2 min to dry the membrane Discard the catch tube and add a new clean 1.5 ml tube 00:02:00
Air-dry the spin-filter for 5 min at room temperature 00:05:00 20 °C, (room temperature)

Add 90 µl 55 °C warm nuclease-free water to the spin filter and gently stir the binding matrix slightly with a pipette, then incubate for at least 5 min 90 µL, 55 °C warm nuc. free water 00:05:00, at least
Centrifuge at 14,000 g for 1 min to get the eluate 00:01:00 Store at 4 °C if you want to stop here 4 °C, don't freeze

Add 31.5 µl magnetic beads (0.35 volume, e.g. AMPure or HighPrep) to 90 µl of DNA sample, shake gently by hand 90 µL, DNA sample 31.5 µL, magnetic beads Around 500 ng/µl DNA is reduced to around 250 ng/µl after cleaning. Alternatively, take less DNA volume and add water if the concentration is over 500 ng/µl
Incubate for 10 min at room temperature 00:10:00 20 °C, room temperature
Spin down and bind to magnet until the liquid is clear,
Stay on the magnet and remove supernatant
(wash 1/2) Add 150 µl Ethanol, don't disturb pellet 150 µL, Ethanol (80 %)
(wash 2/2) Remove supernatant
go to step #27
Spin down, bind to magnet again. Remove the residual liquid with a 10 µl pipette
Add 30 µl nuclease free water and resuspend gently 30 µL, nuclease free water
Incubate for 10 min at room temperature 00:10:00 20 °C, room temperature
Spin down and put on magnetic

Retrieve the supernatant with your DNA Determine dsDNA concentration e.g. via Qubit. Determine DNA length via agarose gel or Bioanalyzer. You should usually lose around half of your total DNA during this process.
Resuspend in 400 µl nuclease free water 400 µL, nuclease free water
creator: Christian Brandt
